name: Grape (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (edible berry, Vitis vinifera, European table grape)

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Reducing hexose; roughly equimolar with fructose in ripe grape; co-dominant fermentable carbon source.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Reducing hexose; slightly exceeds glucose at ripeness. Glucose+fructose account for essentially all grape sugar.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Minor in V. vinifera (unlike American Vitis spp.); rapidly hydrolysed to hexoses.

components.name: tartaric acid
components.source: Rizzon & Miele; Igdir cultivar survey PMC4362841 (4.71-12.71 g/L juice, ~0.5-1.3 g/100g); Vitaceae-specific
components.evidence: Dominant grape organic acid; grape/Vitaceae is nearly the only plant accumulating tartrate at high levels. Midpoint of typical table-grape range; MISSING from prior FooDB profile.

components.name: malic acid
components.evidence: Second major acid; declines during ripening via respiration. Together tartrate+malate = up to 90% of berry organic acids. MISSING from prior FooDB profile.

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence: Minor acid; MISSING from prior FooDB profile.

components.name: succinic acid
components.evidence: Trace organic acid; MISSING from prior FooDB profile.

components.name: L-proline
components.evidence: Co-dominant free amino acid in grape juice/must; major organic-N pool for microbes.

components.name: L-arginine
components.evidence: Highest single amino acid; principal assimilable-nitrogen source in grape fermentations.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Glutamic acid (protein-derived total).

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: Aspartic acid.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: Corrects prior 'D-Serine' entry; food L-serine is the biological form.

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.evidence: Was missing from prior component list; added.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Low; amino acids above are the protein-derived profile.

components.name: vitamin C (L-ascorbate)
components.evidence: Peer-reviewed cultivar data span 11-36 mg/100g (PMC4362841); USDA typical value used.

components.name: folate (B9)
components.evidence: Total folate (DFE).

components.name: glycine betaine
components.evidence: Genuine grape osmolyte; retained.

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Provitamin A carotenoid; Vitamin A RAE ~3 mcg.

components.name: phylloquinone (vitamin K1)
components.evidence: Phytomenadione = vitamin K1.

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.source: FooDB FOOD00369; not in USDA panel
components.evidence: Present but not quantified by USDA; grape reported ~1-3 mcg/100g in some databases. Retained with null.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant mineral cation.

components.name: phosphate (P)
components.evidence: As elemental phosphorus.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Single Fe pool; deduplicates prior triple Fe entries (Fe2+ mito / Fe3+ / Iron).

components.name: sulfate (S)
components.evidence: Sulfur source (present in amino acids + inorganic sulfate); not separately quantified by USDA. Retained with null.

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Counter-ion; grape is low-Cl. Retained with null.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Corresponds to prior '(Z,Z)-9,12-Octadecadienoic acid'. Grape is very low fat (0.16 g/100g).

components.name: palmitic acid (16:0)
components.evidence: Main saturated FA.

components.name: oleic acid (18:1 n-9)
components.evidence: Corresponds to prior 'C18:1, n-9'.

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: Largely non-fermentable pectin/cellulose.

components.name: water
components.evidence: Bulk matrix.

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: Animal-form vitamin A; grapes contain 0 mcg retinol (USDA). Provitamin A is beta-carotene only — retained separately.

remove.name: Pentadecanoic acid
remove.reason: Odd-chain C15:0 is a ruminant/dairy-fat marker; implausible and unmeasured in grape; FooDB predicted noise.

remove.name: Heptanoate
remove.reason: C7 fatty acid is an aroma-volatile trace, not a growth lipid of grape; FooDB noise.

remove.name: Alpha-Carotene
remove.reason: Grape carotenoids are dominated by beta-carotene/lutein; alpha-carotene is negligible/predicted, not measured. Beta-carotene retained.

remove.name: Co2+ (cobalt)
remove.reason: Ultratrace, not quantified for grape and not a defined grape nutrient; FooDB predicted micronutrient noise (cobalt relevant only via B12, absent in plants).

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Ultratrace contaminant element, not a quantified grape nutrient; FooDB predicted noise.

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: Ultratrace element, not quantified for grape; FooDB predicted noise.

remove.name: Molybdate
remove.reason: Ultratrace; not quantified for grape and not growth-defining; FooDB predicted noise.

remove.name: Iodine c
remove.reason: Grape is not an iodine source; ultratrace/soil-dependent, not a defined nutrient; FooDB noise.

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: D-enantiomer is not the food form; corrected to L-serine among amino acids.

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: Duplicate iron pool (intracellular compartment artifact); consolidated into single 'iron' entry.

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: Duplicate iron entry; consolidated into single 'iron' entry.

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: Bacterial-fermentation food preservative (Streptomyces product); not a native grape constituent; FooDB noise.

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: Furanocoumarin characteristic of Apiaceae/citrus, not grape; implausible FooDB predicted entry.

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: Non-biological diastereomeric-mixture artifact of isoleucine; redundant with L-isoleucine.

missing_added: individual sugar amounts (glucose 7.2, fructose 8.1, sucrose 0.15 g/100g)
quantitative amounts for all amino acids, vitamins and minerals
keep_uncovered: genuine grape phytochemicals with no BiGG metabolite; retained in uncovered
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 174683 (Grapes, red or green, European type / Thompson seedless, raw) — full panel incl. sugars, amino acids, fatty acids, vitamins, minerals
Sabir et al. 2013/2015, Sugars, organic acids and phenolics of ancient grape cultivars, PMC4362841 — glucose, fructose, tartaric, malic, citric, succinic, fumaric quantification
Kliewer, Sugars and Organic Acids of Vitis vinifera, PMC1086453 / PubMed 16656357
Frontiers Plant Sci 2024, PMC11499634 — varietal acidity (tartrate:malate ratio)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is the glucose+fructose pair (nearly equimolar, ~15.3 g/100g combined), followed by tartaric acid (the Vitaceae-signature organic acid) and malic acid — the organic acids were entirely absent from the prior FooDB profile and are the key correction here. Arginine and proline dominate the assimilable-nitrogen pool. Fat, fiber and most vitamins/minerals are minor. Values are typical ripe European table-grape (V. vinifera); sugar:acid ratio and vitamin C vary widely by cultivar/ripeness.
